comment: Phase 5.O — Hermes CLI Parity manifest. One entry per upstream hermes_cli/*.py file. Status values: 'ported' (Go port exists and covers tracked scope), 'in_progress' (Go port exists for some scope), 'planned' (no Go port yet), 'not_applicable' (no Go port required — e.g. Python packaging metadata). Keep the 'go' destinations module-relative to gormes/.

files.reason: Python package marker; the Go CLI uses cmd/gormes/version.go for the equivalent __version__ surface.
Go auth subcommand exists; upstream covers many provider-specific flows — broader OAuth breadth remains follow-on.
Subcommand shims share cmd/gormes/auth.go; per-provider commands are collapsed into auth.go for now.
Backup/restore of ${GORMES_HOME} is not yet ported.

Deterministic banner helpers ported: FormatContextLength (tokens→K/M with rounding tolerance), DisplayToolsetName (_tools suffix stripping plus empty-is-unknown), and FormatBannerVersionLabel (pure BannerGitState→title formatting with carried commit/commits pluralization). The Rich/Console renderer, skin-engine color overrides, and subprocess-backed update check remain follow-on scope.
Typer callback plumbing is Python-specific; the cobra tree in cmd/gormes/ uses its own idioms — no direct port needed once commands land.
openclaw migration subcommand remains upstream-only.

Shared print/prompt helpers ported: FormatInfo/Success/Warning/Error/Header wrap Colorize() with the upstream prefixes ("  "/"✓ "/"⚠ "/"✗ "/"\n  ") and color assignments (Dim/Green/Yellow/Red/Yellow), plus FormatPromptLabel ("  <question>[ [<default>]]: " in yellow), ResolvePromptAnswer (strip+default-fallback), FormatYesNoHint (Y/n vs y/N), and ParseYesNoAnswer (empty→default, otherwise startswith 'y' case-insensitively). The interactive stdin/getpass wrapper stays intentionally unported — callers wire their own io.Reader/io.Writer around these deterministic helpers.
Clipboard integration not yet ported.
Codex provider model list lives inside the Go codex client; a dedicated command surface remains follow-on.

ANSI palette (Reset/Bold/Dim/Red/Green/Yellow/Blue/Magenta/Cyan), ColorEnv-driven ShouldUseColor (NO_COLOR / TERM=dumb / TTY precedence), and Colorize() wrapper are ported with deterministic, TTY-free tests.
Top-level command dispatch is shared between cmd/gormes/main.go and the per-subcommand files (auth, doctor, gateway, memory, skills, plugins, acp, session, telegram, version).
Shell completion generation remains upstream-only; cobra supports completions natively but no command is wired yet.
Go config layer ports core settings, XDG paths, and dotenv loading; the full upstream command-driven config surface is broader.
GitHub Copilot auth flow not yet ported.
Go cron engine handles scheduling, persistence, and execution; operator CLI surface (list/add/remove subcommands) remains follow-on.
Curses-based fallback UI remains upstream-only; gormes uses Bubble Tea (internal/tui).
Redacting DebugSession is ported as a tool-side helper; CLI-facing debug subcommand remains follow-on.
The default SOUL.md template ships with the OCI image and Homebrew formula (see Phase 5.P).
DingTalk OAuth flow not yet ported.
Go doctor covers baseline environment + tool checks; full upstream 52k-line doctor (providers, gateway, skills) is incremental.
Debug state-dump subcommand not yet ported.
Dotenv loading replicates upstream precedence with Go-native parsing.
Go gateway chassis + multi-channel manager + operator subcommand are live; upstream gateway.py is 156kB and covers more providers than the current port.
Log tail/query subcommand not yet ported.
Go entrypoint starts the Bubble Tea TUI and wires every registered subcommand; upstream main.py is 310kB and couples many concerns the Go tree splits out.
MCP client and OAuth are ported; an operator-facing mcp-config CLI surface remains follow-on.
Memory bootstrap and CLI command exist; upstream memory_setup covers additional setup wizards.
Model normalization is folded into the Go provider clients; a standalone helper package is not required.
Model catalogue is embedded in the provider clients; an operator `gormes models` subcommand remains follow-on.
Runtime model routing ported to the kernel; an explicit interactive model-switch subcommand remains follow-on.
Nous subscription provisioning flow not yet ported.
Gateway pairing flow is ported inside the gateway package; the CLI-level pairing subcommand is follow-on.

Shared platform registry (ordered Platforms slice with Key/Label/DefaultToolset entries, PlatformByKey lookup, PlatformLabel(key, default) wrapper) is ported with deterministic iteration order matching the upstream OrderedDict for TUI menu stability.
Typed plugin manifest + runtime discovery are ported (Phase 5.I).
Go `gormes plugins list` subcommand ported; install/enable/disable surfaces remain follow-on.
Named-profile subcommand not yet ported.
Provider registry is part of the Go hermes package; a user-facing `providers` subcommand remains follow-on.
Runtime provider routing lives in internal/hermes and internal/kernel; explicit CLI operator surface remains follow-on.
126kB upstream interactive setup wizard not yet ported.
Skills registry + `gormes skills` subcommands are ported (Phase 5.F / 5.F.1 / 5.F.2); broader config surfaces remain follow-on.
Hub sync/install ported (Phase 5.F.1); richer hub browsing UX remains follow-on.
Skin theme engine port is tracked as explicit follow-on scope under Phase 5.Q.
`hermes status` rollup subcommand not yet ported.
Tip-of-the-day subsystem not yet ported.
Tool registry and toolset gating are ported (Phase 5.A/5.J); an operator-facing tools-config subcommand remains follow-on.
Uninstall subcommand not yet ported; Homebrew uninstall handles the brew path.
Generic webhook subcommand not yet ported.
Web dashboard server is explicitly deferred until Phase 7+ per phase-5 scope notes.
